1: A type: "I will sing of steadfast love and justice; to you, O LORD, I will make music." David's royal charter of covenant virtues. Christ as the perfect king embodies both: "God is love" (1 John 4:8) and "must reign until he has put all his enemies under his feet" (1 Corinthians 15:25). Hebrews 2:12 — the risen Christ "sings praise" in the assembly. He fulfills every verse of David's charter without remainder.
2: A type: "I will ponder the way that is blameless. Oh when will you come to me? I will walk with integrity of heart within my house." Hebrews 4:15 — Christ was "in every respect tempted as we are, yet without sin." 1 Peter 2:22 — "he committed no sin, neither was deceit found in his mouth." The longing "when will you come to me?" is the deepest cry of the Psalter — answered in John 1:14 when God comes in the flesh.
3: A revelation of God: "I will not set before my eyes anything that is worthless." Christ set his eyes on the Father's will (John 8:29 — "I always do the things that are pleasing to him"). Hebrews 12:2 — he ran "looking to Jesus, the founder and perfecter of our faith, who for the joy set before him endured the cross." His eyes were fixed on the Father's purpose — he put nothing worthless before his eyes.
4: A revelation of God: "A perverse heart shall be far from me; I will know nothing of evil." 1 John 3:5 — "in him there is no sin." Hebrews 7:26 — "holy, innocent, unstained, separated from sinners, and exalted above the heavens." Christ is the one king who keeps the charter of Ps 101 perfectly — the perverse heart is not merely far from him but has no foothold in him at all. His sinlessness is the ground of our acceptance.
5: A revelation of God: "Whoever slanders his neighbor secretly I will destroy. Whoever has a haughty look and an arrogant heart I will not endure." Matthew 23:12 — "whoever exalts himself will be humbled." Luke 1:51 — God "has scattered the proud in the thoughts of their hearts." Christ denounces pride and slander as incompatible with his kingdom (Matthew 5:21-22; Psalms 23:1-36). He is the king who cannot be moved by arrogance and whose kingdom has no place for it.
6: A shadow: "I will look with favor on the faithful in the land, that they may dwell with me." John 17:24 — "Father, I desire that they also, whom you have given me, may be with me where I am, to see my glory." Revelation 7:15 — "they are before the throne of God, and serve him day and night in his temple." The faithful dwelling with the king and ministering before him is the permanent state of the redeemed in the new creation.
7: A shadow: "No one who practices deceit shall dwell in my house; no one who utters lies shall continue before my eyes." Revelation 21:27 — "nothing unclean will ever enter it, nor anyone who does what is detestable or false, but only those who are written in the Lamb's book of life." Revelation 22:15 — "outside are the dogs and sorcerers and the sexually immoral and murderers and idolaters, and everyone who loves and practices falsehood." The king's house from which liars are excluded is the new Jerusalem.
8: A shadow: "Morning by morning I will destroy all the wicked in the land, cutting off all the evildoers from the city of the LORD." Revelation 22:15 — the evildoers are "outside" the city. Matthew 25:41 — "Depart from me, you cursed." The final purging of evil from God's city is the work Christ will complete at the last judgment — the cutting off that Ps 101 anticipates, the separation of sheep from goats that Matt 25 describes.
